source: Curated by the EnergyDashboard team from IESO Save on Energy, OEB, and Enbridge Gas program pages. Organized by use case — NOT a verbatim mirror. These are 2026 reference points; every 'keyDetail' is marked verify:true until the weekly refresh job reconciles it against source (scripts/programs/). Not affiliated with the IESO, OEB, or any utility.

programs.name: Rate Plan Optimization (TOU vs. ULO vs. Tiered)
programs.useCase: Homeowners time-shifting load (EV charging, pool pumps, laundry)
programs.keyDetail: Ultra-Low Overnight (ULO) prices overnight energy at roughly 3.9¢/kWh versus about 39.1¢/kWh on-peak — a large gap that rewards moving flexible load to nights.

programs.name: Ontario Electricity Rebate (OER)
programs.useCase: General bill relief + reading your bill accurately
programs.keyDetail: An automatic ~23.5% pre-tax credit applied to eligible residential and small-business bills. No application needed; it appears as a line item.

programs.name: Home Renovation Savings Program (HRSP)
programs.useCase: Homeowners planning insulation, heat pumps, or window upgrades
programs.keyDetail: Two paths: a 'Single Upgrade' path (no energy audit required) and a 'Bundled' path that requires a pre- and post-work EnerGuide audit but unlocks larger, stacked rebates.
programs.sourceName: Save on Energy HRSP + Enbridge Gas

programs.name: Peak Perks
programs.useCase: Households with central A/C and a smart thermostat
programs.keyDetail: About $75 to enroll and roughly $20/year to allow small summer peak thermostat adjustments (up to ~2°C). You can override any event.

programs.name: Energy Affordability Program (EAP)
programs.keyDetail: Up to 100% cost coverage for measures like attic insulation, draft-proofing, and efficient appliances for qualifying households.

programs.name: Save on Energy Retrofit Program
programs.useCase: Facility managers upgrading HVAC, lighting, or DERs
programs.keyDetail: Prescriptive incentives for HVAC, lighting controls, and PTHPs. The 2026 update split PTHP tiers (new construction vs. replacement) and adjusted small/medium solar DER incentives.
programs.sourceName: Save on Energy — News and Updates

programs.name: Global Adjustment (GA) Tracking
programs.keyDetail: Not a subsidy — but tracking the monthly Class B GA rate alongside conservation activity is the single highest-value data point for this audience. For Class A exposure, use the GA Exposure tab.

programs.name: Small Business Peak Perks
programs.useCase: Demand response for small commercial sites
programs.keyDetail: Open to businesses with roughly 50 or fewer on-site employees; mirrors the residential smart-thermostat program with summer peak adjustments.
